questions.question: The law of reflection states that the angle of incidence is:
options: Greater than the angle of reflection
Less than the angle of reflection
Equal to the angle of reflection
questions.explanation: The angle of incidence equals the angle of reflection, both measured from the normal to the surface.

questions.question: The image formed by a plane mirror is:
questions.explanation: A plane mirror always produces a virtual, upright image that appears behind the mirror.

questions.question: An object is placed $2.5\text{ m}$ in front of a plane mirror. The image appears to be located:
questions.explanation: For a plane mirror, image distance equals object distance. The image is $2.5\text{ m}$ behind the mirror.

questions.question: When you raise your left hand in front of a plane mirror, your image appears to raise its:
questions.explanation: Plane mirrors produce lateral inversion — left and right are swapped in the image.

questions.question: A virtual image is one that:
options: Can be projected onto a screen
Cannot be projected onto a screen
Is always larger than the object
questions.explanation: Virtual images cannot be captured on a screen because the light rays only appear to come from the image location.

questions.question: Compared to the object, the image in a plane mirror is:
questions.explanation: A plane mirror produces an image that is the same size as the object. Magnification = 1.

questions.question: To see your full body in a plane mirror, the mirror must be at least:
questions.explanation: A mirror half your height, properly positioned with its top at eye level, shows your full body due to the law of reflection.

questions.question: Two plane mirrors are placed at $90°$ to each other. How many images of an object placed between them are formed?
An object is placed $1.5\text{ m}$ from a plane mirror. What is the total distance between the object and its image?
questions.explanation: Object to mirror $= 1.5\text{ m}$, mirror to image $= 1.5\text{ m}$. Total separation $= 3.0\text{ m}$.

questions.question: Which of the following is always true about the image formed by a plane mirror?
questions.explanation: Plane mirror images are always laterally inverted (left-right swapped), virtual, upright, and the same size as the object.

questions.question: If an object is moved $0.5\text{ m}$ closer to a plane mirror, the distance between the object and its image:
questions.explanation: Both object-to-mirror and mirror-to-image distances decrease by $0.5\text{ m}$ each. Total separation decreases by $1.0\text{ m}$.

questions.question: In a ray diagram for a plane mirror, the reflected ray appears to come from:
questions.explanation: The virtual image is located behind the mirror, where the extensions of the reflected rays appear to meet.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered plane mirror reflection!
scoreMessages.70: Good work! Review lateral inversion and the relationship between object and image distance.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing! Focus on virtual image properties and multiple reflections.
scoreMessages.0: Review the basics: law of reflection, plane mirror image characteristics, and object-image distance relationship.
